---
title: "Implementing Bayesian inference"
description: "Tools to quickly build and implement Bayesian models"
categories: 
  - Code
  - Inference
date: 09/02/25
assets-folder: "../../assets/images/notes/bayesian_code"
image: ../../assets/images/notes/bayesian_code/thumbnail.png
# include-in-header:
#     - file: ../../assets/LaTeX/preamble.tex
---

This note was prepared as part of a tutorial on Bayesian inference and Markov Chain Monte Carlo for the [2025 Postdocs in Complexity Conference](https://www.santafe.edu/events/postdocs-in-complexity-global-conference-a-postdoc-tapestry-weaving-global-collaboration) at the Santa Fe Institute. Implementations to follow along with the concepts discussed can be found [here]() in python and R. 

Bayesian inference is a powerful tool for the 

Motivation for Bayesian inference and how it [here](../2025/statistical_inference.html)

A more physical perspective on Markov Chain Monte Carlo [here](../2025/statistical_physics.html#markov-chain-monte-carlo). 

### A simple model
- Suppose that we have a data set, and would like to describe some kind of process that ties the pieces together. 

### Further examples
- Note that if we consider discrete models, many of the tools like STAN that we have discussed start to break down, and we need to put more effort into developing efficient codes.
